Domasi College of Education (DCE), funded by the Malawi Government, replaced the former Domasi Teacher Training College. Domasi College of Education is the largest Teachers\' Training College in Malawi, offering both Diploma and Degree levels of training in secondary education and Degree level training in Primary education.
Learn MoreThe following statistics provide an overview of Domasi's educational landscape, highlighting its student population, faculty strength, academic programs, and alumni network. These figures reflect the institution's commitment to quality education and academic growth.
Embark on a journey of knowledge, discovery, and growth at Domasi College Of Education. Our admissions process is designed to identify bright, motivated individuals who are eager to contribute to our dynamic academic community.

Open Distance Learning
Domasi College of Education is committed to expanding access to quality teacher education through Open, Distance, and e-Learning (ODeL). This flexible mode of learning is designed to cater to aspiring and in-service teachers who may not be able to attend traditional, face-to-face classes due to work or personal commitments
What is ODeL?
ODeL is an innovative approach that integrates online learning, digital resources, and face-to-face interactions to provide an accessible and efficient learning experience. Through this mode, students can engage with course materials, interact with instructors, and participate in discussions from anywhere, making education more inclusive and adaptable to diverse learning needs.
Key Features of ODeL at Domasi College of Education
Flexible Learning
Students can access learning materials anytime and anywhere, both in print and digital mode.
Blended Approach
Combines online learning, printed materials, and occasional face-to-face interaction.
Interactive E-Learning Platform
A dedicated Learning Management System (LMS) for course delivery, assignments, and assessments. (For the future)
Programs Offered via ODeL
Domasi College of Education currently offers degree-level teacher education programs through ODeL. These programs focus on professional development, curriculum implementation, and subject-specific methodologies to enhance teaching effectiveness in Malawian schools. Through ODeL, the College offers programs such as
- Bachelor of Education (Social Sciences)
- Bachelor of Education (Natural and Applied Sciences)
- Bachelor of Education (Language, Arts and Communication)
Benefits of ODeL
Professional Development
Opportunities for in-service teachers to upgrade their qualifications while continuing to work
Cost-effective Learning
Reduces travel and accommodation expenses.
Self-Paced Learning
Encourages self-paced learning, fostering independent study skills
Increased Accessibility
Increased access to higher education for students in remote areas.
How to Apply
Prospective students can apply for ODeL programs by applying to the College when an advertisement is released by the College. Application requirements, deadlines, and guidelines are available Here
For more information, contact the College Registrar at registrar@dce.ac.mw (+265884368543) or the Teacher Education Manager at tem@dce.ac.mw (+265884529603)

It is essential for organizations to demonstrate their active involvement in their local communities. A robust outreach program is an excellent way to showcase this commitment. The Domasi College of Education Strategic Plan (2019-2024) was designed to align the College’s roles with both current and future needs, adapting to an ever-changing environment. Pillar three of the Strategic Plan focuses on Research, Consultancy, and Outreach Activities, and DCE has been actively engaging students and the local community in these initiatives.

Domasi College of Education (DCE) was established in response to a shortage of secondary school teachers in Malawi, which was identified in the early 1990s. Initially, Domasi Teachers’ Training College focused on training primary school teachers, but in 1993, it was upgraded to train secondary school teachers. It started by offering a two-year Diploma in Education, later expanded to a three-year program in 1997, including new departments such as Social Studies. Over the years, the College introduced new faculties, courses, and teaching methods, including open and distance learning (ODL) in 2000 with support from CIDA. It expanded further in 2008 by adding Agriculture Education with funding from the African Development Bank. In 2016, the College
transitioned from diploma programs to Bachelor’s Degrees in Secondary Education, establishing four faculties: Natural and Applied Science, Education, Language Arts and Communication, and Social Sciences. Currently, DCE has 19 academic departments, offers mature entry programs for diploma holders, and has a Centre for Distance and Continuing Education. Enrollment has grown to over 4,000 students. The College is located in Domasi, Zomba, covering 48 hectares, and features modern facilities, including administration blocks, hostels, laboratories, and demonstration schools. DCE operates under the Malawi Government and is affiliated with the University of Malawi. It enjoys some autonomy in academic matters and follows national higher education selection procedures. The College continuously reviews its curriculum to align with local and global education needs and emphasizes research in its programs
WHO WE ARE ?
Our Vision
To become a center of excellence for quality teacher education and continuous professional development.
Our Mission
To train teachers who shall make positive contribution to national development.
Our Mandate
To train secondary school teachers and lecturers for teacher training.
Relations with Other Institutions
The College enjoys cordial relationships with other institutions involved in education in Malawi such as the University of Malawi and Malawi Institute of Education. The College works closely with the University of Malawi, whose Senate approves the programmes and curricula, and final awards to the graduating students. has partnered with a number of universities and stakeholders in various fields. Some of these partners include Michigan State University, UNDP, Norwegian University of Life Sciences, and World Vision Malawi. The College enjoys cordial relationships with other institutions involved in education in Malawi such as the University of Malawi and Malawi Institute of Education.
Do You Have Any Questions Contact With Us
Feel free to contact us for any inquiries or assistance. We're ready to support you every step of the way.

Programme Overview
Our mature entry programme provides a unique pathway for experienced educators to advance their qualifications. Tailored for individuals with a minimum of two years teaching experience and the required diploma credentials, this programme blends academic rigor with practical training.
Entry Requirements
-
a) Possess a Diploma in Education (secondary) obtained from Domasi College of Education or any other accredited institution of higher learning.
b) Alternatively, possess a diploma in relevant content subject areas plus a University Certificate of Education (UCE) or its equivalent obtained from an accredited institution of higher learning.
c) MUST provide evidence that they successfully completed a twelve (12) week Teaching Practice (TP) session as part of their Diploma in Education or UCE studies.
d) Candidates MUST also meet the additional entry requirements for each programme as outlined below.
e) Candidates MUST have a minimum of 2 years teaching experience after obtaining their diploma (for teachers serving in public schools only).
f) Candidates MUST not be more than 50 years of age (for teachers serving in public schools only)
Training Conditions
-
a) Successful candidates shall be required to pay a financial contribution of Three Hundred and Fifty Thousand Malawi Kwacha (MK450,000.00) per academic year as of 2025. However, the fee is subject to revision from time to time.
b) Every successful candidate shall be responsible for payment of Student ID Processing Fee of K15,000.00, Student Affiliation Fee of K2, 000.00 per semester, and Medical Fee of K10, 000.00 per academic year.
c) Successful candidates shall be responsible for their accommodation, meals, and research expenses.
d) Ministry of Education and the College shall not be responsible for any form of allowances to trainees.

STUDENT SUPPORT
DCE realises that coming to College can be stressful. We would therefore like you to join us to learn, relax, and enjoy as you achieve your potential during your studies. You may not need any of the student support services we offer, but it will still be helpful to you to simply know that they exist.
Starting a first-degree programme can be hectic for several reasons. This could be because you are living with complete individual independence for the first time, or you are to cope with a new level of academic rigour. DCE management takes new students through an orientation programme to make the transition from secondary school easier. During the orientation session, you will connect with academic and administrative staff members, register for classes, make friends, learn more about resources available on campus and outside (i.e shopping, accommodation, and banking facilities), and discover ways to get involved in some activities.
RESEARCH AND PUBLICATIONS
Realising the importance of publishing for an academic institution, the College established a research and publications unit. The unit is managed by members of the academic staff appointed by the Principal. The unit coordinates the publications of both academic and non-academic books, written by DCE staff and students, as well as other people within the community. It also coordinates the publications of research work conducted by the College
STUDENT ACCOMODATION
With only 804 bed spaces on campus, students’ accommodation is still very limited. The hostels are divided into male and female categories. The rooms on campus are designed for the occupation of two students, and are furnished with reading tables and chairs, lockers, and beds. You only have to bring your belongings. You will have to apply and be subjected to computer random selection, which removes any bias in the selection process. Males and females are selected separately
You will be required to pay a rental fee per month, per occupant, for the twin room as an undergraduate student. The accommodation fees are revised from time to time by management.
STUDENT HEALTH AND SPRITUALITY
College has well qualified medical personnel. By virtue of being a student at DCE, you are allowed access to medical services at the clin ic at a fee of K10, 000.00 per academic year. Psychosocial counselling: Life at College can be stressful, and students have different abilities of coping with such stress. The College also provides counselling services to students who may experience any form of mental stress, either emanating from academic or social life.
Although DCE is a secular institution, it recognizes individual rights for students to hold their own individual religious engagements. Students are allowed to congregate according to their religious affiliation. Additionally, religious groups are free to book classrooms for their meetings
SPORTS
Whether you are an active sports person, or simply interested in physical fitness or just wanting to socialise, DCE has plenty of opportunities for your comfort. DCE football ground is a full size-11-a-side pitch located in a breeze of Domasi River to the east of the main campus. It is a multipurpose pitch since different markings can be made to accommodate a variety of sporting activities. Additionally, the tracks around the pitch are regularly used in competitive athletics. The College has also basketball, netball and volleyball courts. The College has also a gymnasium purposely built to accommodate various games in a covered arena. Such games include, hockey, basketball, netball, volleyball and tennis, among others
SPECIAL NEEDS
DCE has a Special Needs Unit which is housed close to the library building. Facilities in this section help to increase access to learning materials for students with special needs. For example, this sec tion has facilities such as computers installed with specialised software including Jaws Dongle for the hearing impaired, braille printing machines and screen magnifiers for students with low vision.
Campus Banking
Easily open and manage your bank account within the university.
Internet Connectivity
DCE is one of the few college/university campuses in the country where students and staff have access to WI-FI.

As an esteemed graduate of Domasi College, you are part of a vibrant and accomplished community of alumni. This webpage serves as a platform to celebrate and connect with fellow graduates while also providing valuable resources and opportunities for personal and professional growth.
Do you have a story to share with us? We’re eager to hear about your journey and achievements.
Your support for research, student talent, and facilities impacts not only our university but also the world.
Each year, thousands of students graduate with skills essential for businesses and organizations.
Explore how you can give your time and resources to help current students and engage with the community.

Domasi College of Education Management is pleased to inform all Generic Students and First-year ODeL Students that the End of Semester 1 Examination results have been officially released. Students can access their results on SARIS.
On Thursday, 29th January 2026, a delegation from the French Embassy in South Africa, Lesotho, and Malawi visited Domasi College of Education (DCE) to explore opportunities to strengthen the teaching of French as a foreign language in Malawi. The delegation met with DCE leadership, including Principal Dr. Arkangel Mtipe Yambeni, Deputy Principal Dr. Gilbert Phiri, the Dean of the Faculty of Language, Arts, and Communication, the Academic Registrar, and representatives from the Department of French.
During the discussions, DCE reaffirmed its commitment to expanding French language education by training more teachers and introducing beginner-level courses for students without prior knowledge of the language. The Embassy’s Education and French Cooperation Attaché, Mr. Johann Robinet, and Ms. Inès Schmitt, Cooperation Attaché to Malawi, welcomed these initiatives and pledged support in pedagogy and teaching materials. Both parties also emphasized the importance of establishing a long-term collaboration framework.
The delegation endorsed plans to create a “French Corner” and a resource center at DCE to provide authentic exposure to the French language and culture for students, faculty, and the wider community. Additional discussions with the French Department highlighted staff development opportunities through online and in-person training workshops, subject to funding availability, as part of broader efforts to enhance French language education.
In his remarks, Dr. Yambeni outlined his vision for the Faculty of Language, Arts, and Communication, which includes constructing a dedicated facility for the English, French, and Creative and Performing Arts departments. He appealed for continued support from the French Embassy and the international community to realize this vision.

Our entry requirements ensure that every candidate is well-prepared for the challenges ahead. You must have passed the Malawi School Certificate of Education (MSCE) examinations with at least 6 credits including English. Specific programme requirements are detailed below.
1. Candidates MUST have a minimum of 2 years teaching experience after obtaining their T2
teaching certificate.
2. Candidates MUST have a T2 teaching certificate.
3. Candidates MUST have MSCE, IGCSE, or O-Level certificate or its equivalent with a minimum of credit passes in six subjects including English
4. Candidates with A-Level certificate or its equivalent obtained in the past five years.
5. Candidates MUST have a minimum of credit passes in the two teaching subjects they are
applying for.
6. Candidates MUST not be more than 50 years old at the time of application.
7. Selection does not imply automatic granting of study leave, for which serving teachers MUST apply in line with guidelines and conditions governing this leave.
1. Must hold a valid T2 teaching certificate
2. A minimum of 2 years teaching experience post-T2 certification.
3. Must possess MSCE, IGCSE, or O-Level certificate with credit passes in six subjects (including English).
4. Candidates with A-Level (or equivalent) from the past five years.
5. At least credit passes in both teaching subjects applied for.
6. Applicants must be under 50 years of age.
Note: Selection does not guarantee study leave; serving teachers must apply as per official guidelines.

Advancing Research & Innovation
Shaping the future of education through groundbreaking discoveries and creative solutions.
Our Research Focus
We explore cutting-edge solutions to enhance teaching, learning, and educational policy.
Technology in Education
Integrating AI, digital tools, and e-learning innovations.
Innovative Teaching Methods
Developing data-driven teaching strategies for modern education.
Equity & Inclusion
Ensuring quality education for diverse learning needs.
Recent Research & Innovations
Experiences of non-specialist Social studies teachers in teaching
This is a qualitative study that sought to explore the coping mechanisms of non-specialist Social Studies teachers. The study employed...
Read MoreContribute to Research
Are you working on an innovative project? Submit your research and get featured.
Submit Your Research

Experiences of non-specialist Social studies teachers in teaching
This is a qualitative study that sought to explore the coping mechanisms of non-specialist Social Studies teachers. The study employed the concept of coping as advanced by Lazarus and Folkman (1984). This framework explains that non-specialist Social Studies teachers use adaptive or maladaptive coping mechanism in order to address their inadequacy in content and pedagogy. This study explored the sampled Social Studies teachers’ experiences, challenges and their coping mechanisms in teaching. The following main research question guided the study: What are coping mechanisms of non-specialist Social Studies teachers in CDDSs? To explore this question further, the study answered the following specific research questions: (i) what are the pedagogical experiences of non-specialist Social Studies teachers in CDSSs? (ii) What challenges do non-specialist Social Studies teachers experience in their teaching? (iii) How do non-specialist Social Studies teachers cope with their teaching? Four non-specialist Social Studies teachers were purposively sampled. Data was generated through face-to-face interviews, lesson observations and post-lesson observation interviews. This study found that the sampled non-specialist Social Studies teachers faced challenges in both content and pedagogy. As such, they used adaptive and maladaptive coping mechanisms to deal with their inadequacy. Adaptive coping was due to their orientation on generic issues in teaching while maladaptive coping was due their knowledge gap on the philosophical foundations, aims and goals for the teaching of Social Studies. Based on these findings, it is necessary to address maladaptive coping mechanisms and enforce adaptive coping mechanisms.

2026/2027 Admissions Open: Bachelor of Education (Secondary)
Apply Now to Domasi & Nalikule Colleges of Education
The Ministry of Education, Science and Technology (MoEST) invites suitably qualified Malawians to apply for admission into Bachelor of Education (Secondary) programmes for the 2026/2027 Academic Year at Domasi College of Education and Nalikule College of Education.
These are four-year, face-to-face programmes leading to the award of a Bachelor of Education degree in Sciences, Social Sciences, or Languages, Arts and Communication.
Gender Balance Policy: The Colleges are implementing a 50–50 gender recruitment policy. Female applicants are strongly encouraged to apply.
Minimum Entry Requirements (All Applicants)
Applicants must:
- Possess MSCE, IGCSE, O-Level or equivalent
- Have at least six (6) credit passes, including English / English Language
- Have credit passes in the two teaching subjects applied for
Ineligible Applicants
- Former students withdrawn from public universities or colleges on academic or disciplinary grounds
- T2 teachers dismissed from public service on disciplinary grounds
Application Categories
Category A: School Leavers & Non-Serving T2 Teachers
Applicants must:
- Have obtained MSCE/IGCSE/O-Level between 2021–2025
- Have six (6) credit passes, including English
- Have credits in the two teaching subjects
- Be 45 years old or below
- Meet programme-specific requirements
Category B: T2 Teachers Serving in Public Primary Schools
Applicants must:
- Hold a T2 Teaching Certificate
- Have at least 4 years teaching experience
- Be 50 years old or below
Selection does not automatically guarantee study leave. Serving teachers must apply for study leave according to government regulations.

Each programme has specific subject requirements. Applicants must ensure they meet all subject prerequisites.
Training Conditions & Fees
- Tuition Fee: MWK 350,000 per academic year (subject to revision)
- Other Mandatory Fees:
- Student ID: MWK 20,000
- Student Affiliation: MWK 5,000 per semester
- Medical Fee: MWK 15,000 per year
- No allowances or financial support provided
- All students are self-sponsored
- Admission is non-residential
How to Apply
- Apply online only via: https://www.apply.ac.mw
- Applicants may apply for up to six (6) programmes
- Attach copies of:
- MSCE / IGCSE / O-Level certificates
- A-Level certificate (if applicable)
Additional Documents for Serving T2 Teachers
- T2 Certificate
- First page of GP1 Form or PSR 19
- Recommendation letter from DES / EDM / CEO
Processing Fee
- MWK 15,000 (Non-refundable)
- Pay after receiving an invoice from the application portal
Important Dates
- Application Portal Opens: 9 February 2026
- Application Deadline: 9 March 2026
Late applications will NOT be accepted.
Special Notes
- Applicants with special needs are encouraged to apply (please indicate the nature of the need)
- Applicants to programmes with Human Performance Science must be medically and physically fit
- Applications that do not meet requirements will be disqualified

About The Library
DCE library strives to provide high quality information sources and services through admirable environment with dedicated and well trained personnel.
Vision
To become a centre of excellence in the provision of quality resources and innovative services that stimulate creativity, intellectual curiosity, research and facilitate lifelong learning.
Mission
To provide high quality and innovative library and information services by making accessible scholarly resources to users who shall make positive contribution to national development
Services
Our Services provide students and staff with access to a wide range of academic resources
InterLibrary loan
Library loan periods vary by user category
support staff – 5 books for 30 days
academic staff – 10 books for 30 days
Computer Access
Students and staff can access library computers using a name and password. Wireless connectivity is available for a stable connection.
Photocopying, Spiral Binding & Scanning
The Photocopying & Spiral Binding service offers high-quality photocopying, professional spiral binding, and scanning for documents, making it ideal for students and professionals.
Research
The Research service provides access to a wide range of academic resources to support users in finding reliable information, conducting literature reviews

Fees & Funding
Self-Funding
If you are self-funding your education, it’s important to be prepared to make payments as per the university guidelines. We encourage you to explore flexible payment options to manage your finances effectively.
Loans
For students who may need financial assistance, the Higher Education Student Loans and Grants Board provides an opportunity to apply for loans. Keep an eye on the application window and take advantage of this support if you qualify.
Scholarships
Domasi also accepts scholarships for deserving students. When scholarship opportunities are announced, be sure to apply and increase your chances of receiving financial aid to support your studies. Take the opportunity and apply when the call is open!
